author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
A Treatise on the Gift of Perseverance.
The Preaching of the Gospel and the Preaching of Predestination the Two Parts of One Message.
originalBook: A Treatise on the Gift of Perseverance.
title: The Preaching of the Gospel and the Preaching of Predestination the Two Parts of One Message.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Therefore, by the preaching of predestination, the preaching of a persevering and progressive faith is not to be hindered; and thus they may hear what is necessary to whom it is given that they should obey.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: For how shall they hear without a preacher?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Neither, again, is the preaching of a progressive faith which continues even to the end to hinder the preaching of predestination, so that he who is living faithfully and obediently may not be lifted up by that very obedience, as if by a benefit of his own, not received; but that he that glorieth may glory in the Lord.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: For “we must boast in nothing, since nothing is our own.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: And this, Cyprian most faithfully saw and most fearlessly explained, and thus he pronounced predestination to be most assured.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: For if we must boast in nothing, seeing that nothing is our own, certainly we must not boast of the most persevering obedience.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Nor is it so to be called our own, as if it were not given to us from above.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: And, therefore, it is God’s gift, which, by the confession of all Christians, God foreknew that He would give to His people, who were called by that calling whereof it was said, “The gifts and calling of God are without repentance.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: This, then, is the predestination which we faithfully and humbly preach.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Nor yet did the same teacher and doer, who both believed on Christ and most perseveringly lived in holy obedience, even to suffering for Christ, cease on that account to preach the gospel, to exhort to faith and to pious manners, and to that very perseverance to the end, because he said, “We must boast in nothing, since nothing is our own;” and here he declared without ambiguity the true grace of God, that is, that which is not given in respect of our merits; and since God foreknew that He would give it, predestination was announced beyond a doubt by these words of Cyprian; and if this did not prevent Cyprian from preaching obedience, it certainly ought not to prevent us.
